summary: The first_proof_2 dataset comprises 10 open research-level mathematics problems spanning descriptive set theory, piecewise-linear geometry, probability, and Riemannian geometry. All problems currently show zero open and zero resolved tracked issues, while each carries 100 proof-status entries (q1–q10), indicating substantial proof-attempt activity that is not yet reflected in the issue tracker. No problem has a recorded resolution, so traction cannot yet be differentiated from issue metadata alone.
problems: All 10 problems report 0 open and 0 resolved issues, meaning the issue tracker provides no signal on activity, blockers, or progress.
Each problem has 100 proof-status entries with no corresponding resolved/open issue breakdown, creating a disconnect between proof-attempt volume and tracked outcomes.
Problem titles are truncated (e.g., 'Piecewise-Linear Möbiu', 'Tropical L', 'Proper Proximality fo'), limiting precise scope assessment.
No problem shows any resolved proofs, so none can be confirmed as near-complete from the available data.
The uniform 100-issue proof-status count across all problems prevents distinguishing high-traction from low-traction problems.
highlights: Uniformly high proof-status volume (100 per problem) suggests active, parallel engagement across the entire benchmark batch.
The dataset spans four distinct mathematical domains, offering balanced coverage and opportunities for cross-disciplinary methods.
A clean tracker state (0 open issues) means there is no backlog of unresolved blockers obstructing new work.

suggested_todos.title: Reconcile the 100 proof-status entries per problem with the issue tracker to surface true open/resolved counts
Expand truncated problem titles and tag each with domain and difficulty metadata
Triage the 1000 total proof-status entries to identify problems with the most near-complete proof attempts
Establish a per-problem progress metric (e.g., verified lemmas vs. open gaps) to enable traction comparison
Identify the 2-3 problems closest to resolution and assign focused reviewer attention next week
Audit why resolved-issue counts are zero despite high proof activity to fix tracker pipeline
